title: margaretta rosea subsp. corallina
intro: Margaretta rosea subsp. corallina, also known as the Coral Honeysuckle, is a non-woody climber of the Apocynaceae family, native to the southeastern United States and Mexico, characterized by its bright coral-red flowers. The Coral Honeysuckle has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Margaretta rosea subsp. corallina include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
